items.title: GSF 2.0 mission succeeded in Bringing Gaza issue back to the world stage
items.summary: SEPANG: The increase in global support for the Palestinian struggle and the surge in international coverage are proof of the success of the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 Mission. Sumud Nusantara chef de mission Muhammad Nadir Al-Nuri Kamarulzaman said the mission had succeeded in breaking the "propaganda wall" of Zionist Israel at the international level despite facing various pressures, threats and acts of violence throughout the humanitarian operation. "A total of 12 countries also condemned the actions of the Zionist Israeli pirates who violate human rights and international navigation rights," he said when speaking at the return reception for the GSF 2.0 humanitarian activists at Kuala Lumpur International Airport last night.

items.title: Police say 18‑year‑old missing after separating from family hike at Bukit Asa trail in Selangor
items.summary: Hulu Selangor district police chief ACP Ibrahim Husin said police received information about the incident involving Nur Izzati Humaira Azizul from the Tanjong Malim Fire and Rescue Station at 2.30pm. “The victim was last seen wearing a long brown hijab, a dark green long-sleeved top, black trousers and blue shoes,” he said in a statement today. He said today’s operation involves 51 personnel from various agencies, including the Fire and Rescue Department, police, the Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM), the Social Welfare Department (JKM), mountain guides and volunteers.

items.title: Plan for sports university to support Johor Sports Nation 2030
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Plans are being explored to establish a sports university in Johor as part of efforts to position the state as a high-performance sports hub in South East Asia. State youth, sports, entrepreneur development and cooperatives committee chairman Mohd Hairi Mad Shah said the proposed it would strengthen Johor's broader sports development ecosystem under its Johor Sports Nation 2030 aspirations, the Straits Times reported. "It is a complementary component that can accelerate the state's progress towards becoming an international sports development hub," he said during the Johor state assembly sitting.

items.title: Foreign ministry confirms two Malaysians among victims in deadly Philippines building collapse, one safe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 25 — Malaysia’s Foreign Ministry confirmed that two Malaysians were affected by the building collapse in the city of Angeles, north of the Philippines capital, Manila, on Sunday. The ministry said in a statement that one victim has been confirmed safe, while the Embassy of Malaysia in Manila is actively gathering verified information to ascertain the status of the second Malaysian. Media reports said 26 people were rescued after a nine-storey building under construction collapsed early Sunday in Angeles City.

items.title: Nearly 2,000 luxury vehicles seized in Op Luxury crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Almost 2,000 luxury vehicles have been seized nationwide under Op Luxury as the Road Transport Department (RTD) intensifies enforcement against road tax violations, especially among high-end vehicle owners. "Among the vehicles seized were premium marques, including Porsche and Lamborghini, as well as other high-end models found to have expired road tax. Jazmanie warned that failure to renew road tax is a serious offence under the Road Transport Act, adding that vehicles without valid road tax are liable to compounds and seizure without compromise.

items.title: Three oil rig workers killed, one critically injured in Terengganu lifeboat accident
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 25 — Police have confirmed that three oil rig workers died while another has been critically injured after the lifeboat they were in fell into the sea during maintenance work at the Sepat platform in Terengganu-Kelantan waters yesterday. Kuala Terengganu District Police Chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor identified the deceased as Ahmad Fiqri Zakaria, 38, from Batu Berendam, Melaka; Muhammad Faezuan Hakim Mohammad Bustamam, 28, from Kuantan, Pahang; and Nik Muhammad Hafifi Asri Ab Majid, 38, from Bandar Indera Mahkota, Pahang. All victims were platform safety department personnel who were conducting maintenance on the lifeboat.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against greenback on improved sentiment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar on Monday, supported by improved market sentiment amid investor optimism over possible progress in negotiations between the United States and Iran. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Dollar Index (DXY) fell 0.25 per cent to 98.987 points, while the S&P 500 futures index rose 0.61 per cent to 7,536.50 points, indicating a risk-on sentiment among traders and investors. The ringgit against the US dollar could move towards RM3.95.

items.title: JPJ suspends online vehicle ownership transfers nationwide starting today after fraud complaints
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has announced the suspension of the online service for change of vehicle ownership effective today, encompassing all digital channels, including public portal, kiosks, JPJ business partners MyEG and eAuto Sdn Bhd. JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the immediate control measure was taken following several official and social media complaints about incidents where vehicle ownership was changed without the knowledge of their registered owners. “As such, beginning May 25, all vehicle ownership change matters for individual ownership and private company ownership must be conducted through JPJ counters,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Trump: No rush for Iran deal as US blockade remains in place
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Sunday he had told his representatives not to rush into any deal with Iran, as his administration played down hopes of an imminent breakthrough in the three-month-old war that had been raised a day earlier. But Tasnim news agency, which is linked to Iran's Revolutionary Guards, said the US was still obstructing parts of a potential deal, including Teheran's demand for the release of frozen funds. The US official said Washington envisioned first re-opening the strait and lifting the US naval blockade.

items.title: Oil prices fall over 5pct on hopes for Iran deal
items.summary: TOKYO: Oil prices plunged over five per cent and Asian markets ticked up early on Monday on hopes of a deal to end to the US-Iran war despite President Donald Trump downplaying its imminence. As of around 2300 GMT Sunday, the price of North Sea Brent crude slipped 5.1 per cent to US$98.22 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate fell 5.2 per cent to US$91.57 a barrel before recovering slightly. Japan's Nikkei share index rose more than 2.5 per cent as of around 0030 GMT.

items.title: Stocks rise, oil and dollar slide on Middle East peace hopes
items.summary: SINGAPORE: US stock futures rose on Monday while the dollar and oil prices slipped as the prospect of a deal to end the Iran war buoyed risk appetite although a lack of clarity over when the Strait of Hormuz would open kept enthusiasm in check. Oil prices hit two-week lows to kickstart the week with Brent crude futures down over four per cent to US$98.83 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate CLc1 was at US$92.03 a barrel, also down over four per cent. Nasdaq futures were 0.89 per cent higher and S&P futures were up 0.6 per cent.

items.title: Dollar slumps as signs of deal to reopen Hormuz spur risk appetite
items.summary: Against the yen, the US dollar was down 0.2 per cent at 158.87 yen, while the euro rose 0.3 per cent to US$1.1642 and the British pound gained 0.4 per cent to US$1.3485. The Australian dollar advanced 0.4 per cent to US$0.7160, while its kiwi counterpart tacked on 0.5 per cent to US$0.5877. Oil markets tumbled, as Brent crude prices fell 5.1 per cent to US$98.29 a barrel while US West Texas Intermediate was at US$91.76 a barrel, off 5 per cent.

items.title: Rubio: Hizbollah trying to 'drag Lebanon back into chaos'
items.summary: NEW DELHI: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio accused Hizbollah in a statement Sunday of trying to plunge Lebanon "back into chaos." Rubio denounced what he called Hizbollah's "reckless call to overthrow Lebanon's democratically elected government" and said the pro-Iran armed group was "actively trying to drag Lebanon back into chaos and destruction." Al-Qard Al-Hassan is affiliated with Hizbollah and provides interest-free loans to mainly Shia Muslim communities who have faced financial difficulty amid Lebanon's economic crises.

items.title: Petronas confirms three contractors killed, one injured at FSO Sepat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has confirmed that three contractors were killed while another personnel sustained injuries following an incident during lifeboat maintenance work at FSO Sepat, located at the Sepat field off the coast of Terengganu yesterday. In a statement today, Petronas said the incident occurred at about 12.50pm. "Regrettably, three personnel were pronounced dead upon arrival at Hospital Sultanah Nur Zahirah, Kuala Terengganu, at approximately 5.57pm yesterday, while one injured personnel was evacuated for medical treatment and remains under observation," it said.

items.title: Ex-national footballer, motivational speaker latest to have luxury cars seized
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Luxury vehicles belonging to a former national football player and a motivational speaker were among those seized in a nationwide Road Transport Department (RTD) crackdown on motorists failing to renew their road tax. "The vehicle was valued at more than RM500,000, with annual road tax exceeding RM3,000," he said. Jazmanie said most offenders typically claimed they had "overlooked" renewing their road tax.

items.title: Hospital demand, expansion to lift IHH Q1, says CIMB Securities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IHH Healthcare Bhd is expected to post higher revenue in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), driven by growing inpatient intensity and contributions from its ongoing brownfield expansion projects. In a note, CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd said the healthcare group's top-line growth is likely to remain positive on a year-on-year basis despite Hari Raya Aidilfitri falling 10 days earlier this year. Looking ahead, CIMB Securities expects IHH's operations in Malaysia, India and Turkey to be the main earnings drivers in 2026, supported by expansion initiatives and merger and acquisition opportunities, particularly in Turkey.

items.title: Petronas capex boost, firm oil prices signal OGSE upside
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A potential increase in Petroliam Nasional Bhd's (Petronas) capital expenditure (capex) by 2027 could provide a fresh boost to Malaysia's oil and gas services and equipment (OGSE) sector, supported by elevated Brent crude oil prices and rising energy security investments across the region. In a research note, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said Petronas' capex has historically exhibited a 51 per cent correlation with Brent crude prices, typically with a one-year lag due to project lead times and disciplined capital allocation. The firm noted that during the 2012-2014 period, when Brent crude averaged about US$107 per barrel, Petronas maintained annual capex of between RM57 billion and RM65 billion.

items.title: Sarawak Plantation earnings seen sensitive to CPO price swings, says PublicInvest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sarawak Plantation Bhd is expected to remain highly sensitive to movements in crude palm oil (CPO) prices, with every RM100 change in CPO prices estimated to affect the group's bottom line by about RM8 million, according to Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest). PublicInvest noted that this accounted for 15.1 per cent and 16.6 per cent of the firm and the street's full-year expectations, respectively. Looking forward, PublicInvest said despite seeing a moderate seven per cent growth in Q1 FY26, Sarawak Plantation retains its strong FFB production growth target of 25 per cent for FY26F, with sharp production growth starting from May.

items.title: Chartered or forgotten: IPRM must now choose
items.summary: The Institute of Public Relations Malaysia (IPRM) is at a crossroads. As AI rewrites content workflows, new publicity rules reshape regulated professions, and a new generation questions everything from ethics to ESG, Malaysia needs a PR institute that is bold, outward-facing and future-ready, not just ceremonial. From club to chartered guardian Formed in 1962, IPRM has been the national professional body for PR for more than six decades.

items.title: Turkiye president scraps move to close liberal university
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Turkiye's President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Sunday reversed his controversial decision to close a liberal private university in Istanbul, according to a decree published in the official gazette. Hundreds of students and lecturers demonstrated earlier in the day outside the main campus of Istanbul Bilgi University, which has been closed since Friday, to demand its reopening. Erdogan on Friday ordered its closure in the middle of the school year, in an official decree.

items.title: Beyond data centres: Can Malaysia truly become an AI nation by 2030?
items.summary: Indeed, few countries can afford to remain passive as AI begins transforming manufacturing, finance, logistics, healthcare, education and public administration simultaneously. Yet the more important question is not whether Malaysia can become AI-ready. Malaysia possesses several important structural advantages.

items.title: Ikea Malaysia keeps prices steady despite global cost pressures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ikea Malaysia is holding prices steady despite rising global cost pressures and is also brushing off competition from online marketplaces, betting on quality and affordability to sustain demand. Country retail director Malcolm Pruys said the company has no plans to raise prices, even as geopolitical uncertainties and supply chain disruptions push up logistics costs globally. Pruys said the long-term implications remain uncertain but noted that Ikea Malaysia has a sufficiently large and resilient supply chain to navigate current challenges.

items.title: Rising oil prices threaten to squeeze Malaysia's construction sector as costs climb [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and the rise in crude oil prices are expected to intensify cost pressures on Malaysia's construction and property sectors over the next six to 12 months, with steel, cement, logistics and fuel costs all moving higher. Industry players warn that sustained energy price volatility could affect project viability, particularly for contractors operating under fixed-price contracts, where rising input costs are difficult to pass on. Zerin Properties chief executive officer Previndran Singhe said construction costs are already rising as energy remains deeply embedded throughout the building supply chain.

items.title: Trigger mechanism could soften fuel subsidy shock: Economists [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A transparent fuel price "trigger mechanism" that adjusts retail prices when global oil prices breach predetermined thresholds could help cushion economic shocks while reducing long-term subsidy leakages. Economists said without a phased approach, even a modest fuel price hike of between 10 sen and 20 sen per litre could trigger widespread spillover effects. Transport costs, fresh food prices, e-hailing fares and operating expenses for small businesses will soar, they said.

items.title: Sources: Hamzah's 'Reset' among top agenda in Pas meeting
items.summary: KOTA BARU: At its emergency central committee meeting yesterday, Pas discussed, among others, an alleged movement among Bersatu's grassroots supporting Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's "Reset" team. "I was told that several state commissioners raised the issue with the party's top leadership, saying Bersatu's grassroots support appeared to be shifting towards the its former deputy president. Meanwhile, PN deputy chairman and Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man confirmed that the meeting had discussed the party's relationship with Bersatu, following recent remarks by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.

items.title: Expert warns prolonged Kedah planting delay may disrupt rice production [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: If the padi planting delay in Kedah extends into June or July — more than one to two months behind schedule beginning from mid-April to mid-May — the disruption could significantly affect national production, said a food security expert. Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM) senior research fellow Associate Professor Dr Bakri Mat said while reports of the delay were concerning, they could not yet be interpreted as evidence of a looming supply crisis, as other data had suggested more than half of padi farmers in the state were already engaged in ploughing and field preparations. His comments come in response to figures from Malaysian Padi Farmers Association (PeSAWAH) indicating that only about 10 per cent of Kedah's 56,000 padi farmers — including those in the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) area — had begun planting, even though the dry-season schedule was supposed to have started last month or early this month.

items.title: Properly regulated sex offender registry can boost public safety, says criminologist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia can significantly boost public safety without infringing on human rights by establishing a properly regulated sex offender registry, said a criminologist. He said Malaysia could easily tailor its own registry to suit local needs rather than duplicating foreign models wholesale. Sundramoorthy said registries were preventive risk-management tools designed for harm reduction, rather than public humiliation.

items.title: 117 held in multi-year child predator crackdown [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's multi-year crackdown on online child predators has reached a major milestone, with four successive operations resulting in 117 arrests and at least 101 charges. The operations involved the processing of more than 1.4 million digital files containing Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM). Professor Dr Selvakumar Manickam, director of the Cybersecurity Research Centre at Universiti Sains Malaysia, described the shift in alarming terms.

items.title: When communities take charge: How once-neglected lift lobbies in two PPRs now serve as bustling community hubs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Lift lobbies in most People’s Housing Projects (PPR) seldom age well. A newly-transformed lift lobby in PPR Lembah Subang 1 under the K2K initiative. — Picture courtesy of Think City Against this backdrop, Think City pushed for residents in PPR Kg Baru Hicom and PPR Lembah Subang 1 — which house almost 19,000 people combined — to reclaim their lift lobbies.

items.title: Turkiye riot police use tear gas to take opposition party HQ
items.summary: ANKARA: Hundreds of Turkish riot police firing teargas forced their way into the Ankara headquarters of the country's main opposition party on Sunday, days after a court had dismissed its leadership, AFP journalists saw. Party members had blocked the building's entrances, defying the court order issued Thursday as part of an official probe against the Republican People's Party (CHP), before officers broke in to remove the group's leader. "They stormed our headquarters, used tear gas, beat us with batons, ransacked the party (building) and threw us out," Ozel told AFP on Sunday evening.

items.title: China launches three-person space mission in push for Moon landing
items.summary: BEIJING: China launched its crewed Shenzhou-23 spacecraft and eased it into a successful docking with a space station early Monday as part of Beijing's ambitions to send humans to the Moon by 2030, state media said. During this mission, a Chinese astronaut is scheduled to spend a full year in orbit on the Tiangong space station, a crucial first in the Chinese lunar landing programme. The spacecraft separated from the rocket around 10 minutes later and entered orbit, the Chinese Manned Space Agency (CMSA) said on social media.

items.title: Trump tempers expectations of a Middle East deal with Iran
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump tempered expectations of a Middle East deal by saying on Sunday he had told his negotiators not to "rush", even after both Teheran and Washington signalled signs of progress towards an agreement to end the war. The United States and Iran have observed a ceasefire since April 8 while mediators push for a negotiated settlement, although Iran has imposed controls on Gulf shipping and the US has blockaded Iran's ports. Iran's Lebanese ally Hizbollah attacked Israel on March 2 after US-Israeli strikes killed Iran's supreme leader.

items.title: 'Social media age limit rooted in duty of care'
items.summary: The proposed 16 years minimum age for opening social media accounts is a preventive measure rooted in the principle of duty of care. Zulaikha Mat Zin of the Messrs Aimee and Zaim Arif law firm said minors were highly exposed to cybercrime, including sextortion and cyberbullying. She said Malaysia had a comprehensive legal framework, including the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 and Evidence Act 1950 that includes digital evidence.

items.title: Tun Mustapha Park poised to get global recognition
items.summary: After a decade of marine conservation efforts, community involvement and sustainable marine management, Sabah's Tun Mustapha Park is now close to getting a prestigious global recognition. "Tun Mustapha Park has also been registered under the Coral Triangle Marine Protected Area System Category 3 as a 'Priority Development Site'. Lintar said the park, gazetted on May 19, 2016, was Malaysia's largest multi-use marine park, covering the waters of Kudat, Kota Marudu and Pitas.

items.title: Ringgit expected to trade at 3.95-3.97 against US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to trade within a narrow range of between 3.95 and 3.97 against the US dollar amid a shortened trading week. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the focus would be on the United States' personal consumption expenditures (PCE) data for April. Afzanizam said while the ringgit is expected to move in a narrow range, Malaysia's macroeconomic indicators are fairly decent.

items.title: Aidiladha, Wesak, Kaamatan, Gawai: Malaysia’s massive festive mash-up is about to take over
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Malaysia is set to witness an “extraordinary festive atmosphere” soon as people of various races and religions celebrate several major festivals falling almost simultaneously during the school holiday season. The uniqueness of Malaysia’s diversity will be clearly on display as Muslims celebrate Hari Raya Aidiladha on May 27, Buddhists observe Wesak Day (May 31), while the Kadazandusun and Dayak communities celebrate the Kaamatan Festival (May 30 and 31) and Gawai Day (June 1 and 2), respectively. She said although bus and train ticket prices tend to rise during the holiday season, the opportunity to return home and reunite with family remains worthwhile.

items.title: Bersatu details failed talks with PAS over Negeri Sembilan political crisis
items.summary: In a lengthy public statement on Facebook posted tonight, the Bersatu political bureau said talks involved efforts to manage cooperation among Perikatan Nasional (PN) assemblymen and their relationship with Umno representatives in the state government. The Bersatu politburo said its leadership made several attempts to engage PAS between late April and early May 2026 to discuss the matter. Between April 28 and May 5, 2026, PAS did not respond to Bersatu’s proposals, the politbiro said.

items.title: Perikatan tensions surface as Bersatu issues detailed rebuttal to PAS chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Bersatu tonight issued an open statement in response to PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang following remarks touching on internal Perikatan Nasional (PN) disputes involving the coalition’s member parties. The bureau said it was compelled to clarify the matter after PAS made public statements, stressing that it was responding to avoid misinterpretation of Bersatu’s position. “However, since the PAS president has made public statements on the matter, the Bersatu Political Bureau is of the view that these issues must be clarified publicly to avoid misunderstandings and negative perceptions of Bersatu among party members and the public,” it said.

items.title: No Bersatu-Pakatan deal in sight, DAP sec-gen says amid coalition rumours
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 24 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today dismissed speculation that Bersatu could join Pakatan Harapan amid talk of strains in its relationship with PAS. Rumours of possible friction within Perikatan Nasional (PN) have surfaced in recent days following speculation that PAS may reconsider its political ties with Bersatu. The Seremban MP said he is not interested in entertaining speculation over possible political realignments involving Bersatu.

items.title: ‘We do not have a choice’: DAP sec-gen cites trust issue in fresh push for Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 24 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today said the Negeri Sembilan legislative assembly should be dissolved to allow voters to decide on a fresh state mandate following Barisan Nasional’s (BN) withdrawal of support for the Pakatan Harapan-led state government. Loke, who is also Seremban MP and transport minister, said he made the proposal during the PH convention in Johor last Sunday for the Negeri Sembilan assembly to be dissolved simultaneously with the Johor legislative assembly. He said PH is prepared to face a fresh state election if consent for dissolution is granted by the Negeri Sembilan Ruler.

items.title: One brother found dead, search continues for younger sibling missing in Dungun river
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 24 — The body of a 15-year-old boy, believed to have drowned while swimming with his brother in Sungai Beril near Taman Beril, Paka, this morning, was found this afternoon. Acting Dungun police chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said the body of Muhammad Amir Ashraf was found by a search and rescue (SAR) team at 3.47 pm. He said divers from the Chukai Fire and Rescue Department’s Water Rescue Team found the body of the Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama Durian Guling student on the riverbed at a depth of about 5.1 metres.

items.title: ‘Fight Like Jalil’: Queen launches Malaysia’s new warship named after late son
items.summary: ISTANBUL, May 24 — Malaysia today marked a historic milestone in strengthening its maritime defence capabilities with the naming and launching ceremony of the Royal Malaysian Navy’s (RMN) first Littoral Mission Ship Batch 2 (LMSB2) at a shipyard here. Also present were Tunku Temenggong of Johor Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim, Tunku Panglima of Johor Tunku Abdul Rahman Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim and Tunku Putera of Johor Tunku Abu Bakar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim. The naming of the first LMSB2 vessel as Tunku Laksamana Abdul Jalil is the highest tribute to the fourth child of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Raja Zarith Sofiah, the late Tunku Abdul Jalil, who was the first member of the Johor royal family in modern history to hold the title of ‘Laksamana’ (Admiral).

items.title: ‘I lost my grip’: Teen recounts nightmare moment as brothers swept away in Dungun river
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 24 — Friends of two siblings, Muhammad Amir Ashraf Mohd Zul-Zunaidi, 15, and Muhammad Aidil Amsyar Mohd Zul Zunaidi, 14, expressed their devastation over being unable to save them when the brothers were swept away by swift currents as they were bathing in Sungai Beril near Taman Beril, Paka here today. Muhammad Afi Naashif Adli Noone,15, said he and his friend Muhammad Farish Iqbal Cik Mohana Runasdi, 14, tried their best to rescue the brothers after they saw them struggling in the water as the current swept them away at 10.56 am. The boy said he tried to reach Muhammad Amir while Muhammad Farish Iqbal rushed to help Muhammad Aidil.

items.title: Sabah PKR says Semporna, Tuaran divisions staying loyal despite chiefs quitting
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 24 — Semporna and Tuaran PKR divisions will continue to support the reform agenda despite the resignations of their respective chiefs, said PKR Sabah information chief Ahmad Farid Sainuri. He said both divisions, now formally led in an acting capacity by their respective deputy chiefs, continue to champion the rights of the people of Sabah, particularly on the implementation of the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) and broader institutional reforms. PKR Semporna division chief Mohd Amin Abdul Mem, who is also PKR State Leadership Council vice-chairman, and PKR Tuaran division chief Mohammad Razeef Rakimi have recently announced their resignation from all party posts and their departure from the party.

items.title: Mum claims primary school bullies broke son’s arm, Kedah Education Dept investigating
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 24 — The Kedah Education Department (JPN) is investigating the alleged bullying of a primary school pupil in the state, following media reports on the matter yesterday. State education director Abdul Rahim Mat said the department views the allegations seriously and guaranteed a transparent and thorough investigation to ensure justice for the victim. Media reports previously highlighted that a nine-year-old boy was allegedly bullied in three separate incidents at a primary school in the Kubang Pasu district.

items.title: Asked about Pandan candidacy for GE16, Tengku Zafrul says decision rests with PKR leadership
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz today said PKR’s leadership will decide whether or not he contests the Pandan parliamentary seat in the 16th general election (GE16). The prime minister’s senior political adviser said the matter was up to PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, The Star reported. Tengku Zafrul said he has been working closely with the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council to address issues affecting residents in Pandan.

items.title: Wet and stormy weather for Selangor, Johor, Sabah, Sarawak and peninsular east coast tonight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) today issued a thunderstorm warning for several states across peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak until 7pm. MetMalaysia said the warning involves areas in Perak, Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Negeri Sembilan and Johor. The warning in Kelantan covers Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Kota Bharu, Bachok, Machang and Pasir Puteh.

items.title: Putrajaya reports no new deaths from Covid-19 this year as Singapore battles fresh wave
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — The current Covid-19 situation in Malaysia remains stable and has shown a significant downward trend compared to last year, with no deaths recorded so far this year. The Ministry of Health (MOH), in a statement today, said although neighbouring countries have recently reported an increase in cases, the number of Covid-19 cases in the country up to Epidemiological Week (EW) 19/2026 had declined by 12.6 per cent (9,682 cases) compared to the same period last year. “Although there was a 0.7 per cent increase (575 cases compared to EW18/2026), the situation remains under control.

items.title: Police arrest eight, seize ketamine and ecstasy in KL hotel raid linked to a sudden death case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Police arrested eight individuals of various nationalities for alleged drug abuse in a raid at a popular hotel on Jalan Stesen Sentral here this morning. Brickfields police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said the raid was conducted following the death of a 31-year-old local man who had been brought from the hotel to the emergency department of Kuala Lumpur Hospital yesterday morning. Hoo said that acting on information obtained, a team from the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (JSJN) of the Brickfields district police headquarters raided the victim’s hotel room at about 4 am today.

items.title: National Youth Day 2026 draws families and youth with games, music and cultural activities
items.summary: Revathi A. Suntharamurthy, 23, from the Seputeh Hindu Youth Organisation, said the programme stood out for its interactive format and strong focus on youth empowerment. Revathi A. Suntharamurthy said the programme’s interactive approach and emphasis on youth empowerment allowed young people from diverse backgrounds to come together and build connections through shared experiences. — Picture by Raymond Manuel She said it created space for young people from different backgrounds to come together and develop through shared experiences.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines receives 200th Boeing aircraft with latest Boeing 737-8 delivery
items.summary: SEPANG, May 24 — Malaysia Airlines today received a new Boeing 737-8 aircraft, marking the 200th Boeing aircraft delivered to the national carrier since 1972. To date, MAG has received 18 Boeing 737-8 aircraft from its total order of 55 Boeing narrowbody aircraft. The order comprises 43 Boeing 737-8 aircraft and 12 Boeing 737-10 aircraft scheduled for delivery through 2030.

items.title: Tengku Hassanal directs Armed Forces to seek Pahang Sultan’s consent for any military drills on state islands
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — The Pahang crown prince has decreed that the Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM) and all relevant agencies must first inform and obtain the Sultan of Pahang’s consent before conducting any military training or exercises in the state. In a statement issued by Istana Abdulaziz in Kuantan and released to the media, Tengku Mahkota Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah said he was paying close attention to the proposed exercises and their potential impact. “His Royal Highness expressed concern that the military activities could disrupt the natural ecosystem, flora and fauna of Pahang,” the statement said.

items.title: Counselling, early intervention keeping domestic‑violence cases under control in Melaka, says state exco
items.summary: MELAKA, May 24 — Counselling sessions and advisory services provided by the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN) and the Social Welfare Department (JKM) continue to play an important role in assisting women, especially mothers, who are facing domestic violence and abuse. Melaka Women, Family and Community Development Committee chairman Datuk Kalsom Noordin said more women are now coming forward to seek help and support through the platforms provided by the agencies to resolve domestic conflicts in a safer and more structured manner. She said the counselling sessions not only provide emotional support to victims, but also offer guidance on legal rights, protection and actions that can be taken in cases involving domestic abuse or mistreatment.

items.title: Fire Dept: Two teens feared drowned after being swept away in Sungai Beril
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 24 — Two brothers are feared drowned while swimming with two friends in Sungai Beril, Paka, here today. Paka Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Mohd Rizuan Ramli said Muhammad Amir Ashraf Mohd Zulnaidi, 15, and Muhammad Aidil Amsar Mohd Zulnaidi, 14, from Kampung Kolam here, were believed to have been swept away by strong currents while swimming in the river at about 11 am. Mohd Rizuan said the four students had gone to the river for a swim at about 10.30 am.

items.title: Fahmi: Media organisations should be able to earn from social media livestreams, govt to engage TikTok on monetisation rules
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said Putrajaya is prepared to engage with TikTok over possible monetisation mechanisms for Malaysian media organisations. He said the move followed concerns raised by media industry representatives that their organisations were unable to generate income on some social media platforms, even when their official content attracted significant viewership. Fahmi said the discussions would look at possible solutions for Malaysian media organisations, particularly those with official accounts producing high-performing live content.

items.title: Company director in Kota Tinggi loses RM167,050 after responding to fake loan ad on Facebook
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, May 24 — A company director lost RM167,050 after allegedly falling victim to a non-existent loan syndicate. He said the victim was lured by a personal loan advertisement on May 21 offering fast approval, before being instructed to make several payments purportedly for loan processing and approval. Yusof said the victim realised he had been scammed after failing to receive the promised loan and later lodged a police report.

items.title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry deploys four financing agencies to engage MSMEs on the ground, in line with PM’s call
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 24 — The Ministry of Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives (Kuskop) is mobilising four of its financing agencies to go to the ground and engage micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) by providing advisory services and direct financing assistance. Its Minister, Steven Sim, said the four agencies are the National Entrepreneur Group Economic Fund (TEKUN Nasional), Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM), SME Bank and Bank Rakyat, which are regularly deployed, including during the nationwide Kuskop Madani Sales programme. Yesterday, Anwar called on relevant agencies to intensify efforts to go to the ground and provide information to small and medium enterprises (SMEs) on financing facilities and assistance offered by the government.

items.title: Latest Ebola outbreak now third largest in history — is Malaysia at risk?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The latest Ebola outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda are now the third largest in history, with nearly 750 suspected cases and almost 90 confirmed cases reported within weeks of being declared. The largest Ebola epidemic happened between 2014 and 2016 in West Africa, with 28,600 recorded cases, while the second biggest outbreak also took place in the DRC, where over 3,470 cases were recorded between 2018 and 2020. On May 17, the World Health Organisation declared the outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).

items.title: Fahmi announces June 1–30 application window for RM5m Madani content fund, extra RM100,000 for youth programmes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today announced that the application for the Madani Generation Catalyst Fund will open from June 1 to June 30, 2026, with RM5 million allocated to support local creative content production. He explained that the fund is open to eligible local companies and youth organisations. “The fund is open to eligible local companies and youth organisations for the production of five key categories which are telefilms, documentaries, short films, short documentaries and animation, with a total allocation of RM5 million,” he said in his speech during the closing ceremony of the Youth Festival at National Youth Day 2026 for the Federal Territories here today.

items.title: Body of 11-year-old boy reported missing near NKVE found at Sungai Buloh riverbank
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — An 11-year-old boy reported missing near a river by the NKVE close to Menara Finas in Damansara Damai, Petaling Jaya, was found dead this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the boy’s body was found by the riverbank at Jalan Welfare, Kampung Baru Sungai Buloh. The search resumed this morning along the riverbank from Paya Jaras Hilir Bridge to Merbau Sempak Bridge, before the boy was found.
